title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people sharing how learning a language changed their perspective on culture. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Anna, 25, Translator
[43]Learning Spanish opened up a whole new world for me[/43]. I started to understand jokes and traditions that don’t exist in my own culture. [47]It made me realise how language shapes the way people think[/47]. [50]Now, I feel at home in two countries[/50].
texts.B:
### Peter, 31, Engineer
[44]I learned Japanese for work, and it changed how I see politeness and respect[/44]. [48]I noticed that some ideas are difficult to translate because they’re so tied to culture[/48]. [51]I’ve become more open-minded about different ways of living[/51].
texts.C:
### Maria, 28, Teacher
[45]Studying French helped me appreciate art and literature in a new way[/45]. [49]I love being able to connect with people in their own language[/49]. [52]It’s made me more curious about other cultures[/52].
texts.D:
### Luca, 36, Chef
[46]I moved to Germany and had to learn the language quickly[/46]. [47]It was tough, but now I feel proud of how far I’ve come[/47]. [49]I enjoy discovering local customs and food[/49].

questions.question: felt that learning a language gave them access to a new world?
questions.explanation: Anna says learning Spanish opened up a whole new world.

questions.question: gained a new understanding of politeness through language study?
questions.explanation: Peter learned Japanese and saw politeness differently.

questions.question: developed a deeper appreciation for art and literature?
questions.explanation: Maria appreciated art and literature more after studying French.

questions.question: had to learn a language quickly after moving abroad?
questions.explanation: Luca moved to Germany and had to learn the language quickly.

questions.question: realised that language influences how people think?
questions.explanation: Anna realised language shapes the way people think.

questions.question: found some concepts hard to translate due to cultural differences?
questions.explanation: Peter noticed some ideas are hard to translate because they’re tied to culture.

questions.question: enjoys learning about local traditions and food?
questions.explanation: Luca enjoys discovering local customs and food.

questions.question: feels equally at home in two countries?
questions.explanation: Anna feels at home in two countries.

questions.question: has become more accepting of different lifestyles?
questions.explanation: Peter has become more open-minded about different ways of living.

questions.question: became more curious about other cultures through language learning?
questions.explanation: Maria became more curious about other cultures.
